methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $157M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 12 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $18.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Quantum-Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $8B

data.narrative:
BRINC Drones, founded by Blake Resnick, is a Series C-stage defense and security company that has raised $157M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 12 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, comprising 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 10 private firms including Quantum-Systems, Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression methodology, using total capital raised as a valuation proxy, applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yielding $267M. The peer-median approach across the 12-company Defense & Security cohort produces a median valuation of $1.8B, with a observed range from $300M to $18.0B. Sample peers include Quantum-Systems at $8.0B (Series C), Helsing at $18.0B (Series D), and Saronic at $9.2B (Series D).

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $227M to $2.0B, with a median implied value of $1.0B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around BRINC's market positioning relative to peers. Stage-progression yields a conservative floor, while peer multiples suggest substantially higher value if BRINC's growth trajectory and defensibility align with top-quartile comparables. Key uncertainties include competitive differentiation within the autonomous defense sector and pathway to near-term revenue inflection.
